title: springy tunisian beets tagine
description: a vibrant and flavorful stew originating from tunisia, perfect for springtime. this recipe is dairy-free-friendly and features a medley of beets, aromatic spices, and a hint of sweetness.
instructions: heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
add a minced clove of garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
add a diced onion, carrot, and celery and cook for 5 minutes, until they start to soften.
add 2-inch cubes of golden beets and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
pour in 2 cups of chicken broth and 2 tablespoons of apple cider vinegar, scraping up any browned bits from the pan.
add 1 teaspoon each of ground cumin, coriander, paprika, and cinnamon, along with a pinch of salt.
bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes, or until the beets are tender.
stir in some chopped fresh parsley and a squeeze of lemon juice.
season with additional salt and pepper, if needed.
serve the tagine over couscous or rice, garnished with additional parsley and lemon wedges.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
